suggestions.title: Keyboard shortcuts toast fires on mobile — makes zero sense
suggestions.description: ShortcutToast shows '⌨️ Shortcuts: 1 Office · 2 Dashboard · R Refresh · Esc Clear' on first visit. On a phone, none of those shortcuts work. Worse, the persistent bottom bar also renders the same shortcut text at the bottom of the mobile viewport, eating real estate. Fix: wrap ShortcutToast render in a `window.matchMedia('(hover: none)').matches` check (same device detection already used elsewhere in Character), and hide the shortcuts bottom bar on mobile via CSS `@media (pointer: coarse)`. Additionally, the 'move mouse to refresh' copy in the pollingPaused pill also leaks desktop assumptions onto mobile — change to 'Tap to refresh' on touch devices.

suggestions.title: Duplicate Sofia ghost — idle character renders at desk AND wandering
suggestions.description: When a worker is idle, the Office component maps over DESKS and renders a desk with '↪ away' nameplate (including the Character in ghost-desk logic), AND separately renders a wandering Character via the idleWorkers.map(). Result: Sofia appears twice on screen with the same name label, which is visually baffling on first glance. The fix is clear: idle workers should NOT get a standing Character at the desk position — their desk shows the nameplate/away sign, but only the wandering Character should be rendered. Remove the desk Character rendering for idle workers OR suppress it when `occ.idle === true` (the desk slot assignment already marks it, just skip the Character render for those slots).

suggestions.title: stuckWorkers useMemo captures Date.now() at memoize time — never re-evaluates
suggestions.description: In StatsBar: `const stuckWorkers = useMemo(() => workers.filter(w => ... (Date.now() - new Date(w.updated_at).getTime()) > 7_200_000), [workers])`. Because Date.now() is evaluated inside the memo and the dependency is only [workers], a worker can become 'stuck' (2h elapsed) without the 'Stuck' card ever appearing until the next API poll updates the workers array. Fix: add a tick value from useTimeTick to the deps array, or compute stuckWorkers directly in render (it's a simple filter, memoization here is premature). Related: same issue affects the `formatDuration` duration tier calculation inside WorkerCard's inline IIFE.

suggestions.title: CSS selector injection via worker name in querySelector
suggestions.description: In StatsBar's onClick for stuck workers: `document.querySelector(\`[data-worker-name="${name}"]\`)`. If a worker's name contains a double-quote, backslash, or bracket character, this throws a SyntaxError or matches incorrectly. This is a React anti-pattern AND a latent bug. Fix: use `CSS.escape(name)` — i.e., `document.querySelector(\`[data-worker-name="${CSS.escape(name)}"]\`)`. Better long-term fix: lift a `workerCardRefs` Map from App.jsx and pass a ref callback into WorkerCard so StatsBar can call `refs.get(name)?.scrollIntoView()` directly without touching the DOM.

suggestions.title: completionToast uses a wall of inline styles instead of a CSS class
suggestions.description: The completion toast (`🎉 {name} shipped it!`) and the plant toast (`🌱 The office ficus leveled up!`) are both rendered with ~15 inline style props each (position, background, border, borderRadius, padding, fontSize, fontWeight, color, boxShadow, zIndex, whiteSpace, pointerEvents, animation). This is inconsistent with the rest of the codebase which uses CSS classes/variables. These toasts should be extracted into a reusable `<Toast>` component with a className like `toast toast--completion` and `toast--plant`, with styles in App.css. This also makes theming easier — currently these toasts ignore the light theme's --surface2 variable because the background is hardcoded.

suggestions.title: Office canvas is not usable on mobile — no scroll, labels invisible
suggestions.description: On 375px viewport, the office floor is scaled down to fit, making desk nameplates, monitor screens, and character names extremely small (unreadable). There's no touch-scroll or pinch-zoom affordance. The worker name labels ('Sofia', 'Carlos', etc.) are approximately 6-7px tall at this scale. Two options: (1) Add `touch-action: pan-x pan-y` plus `overflow: auto` to `.office-floor` and give it a fixed minimum width (e.g., 600px) so users can scroll/pan it. (2) Show a simplified list view on mobile instead of the canvas. Option 1 is faster. Also consider a 'tap to zoom' affordance or a small '🔍 pinch to explore' hint the first time.

suggestions.title: Double slice(-3) in WorkerCard back panel — only ever shows ≤3 entries regardless
suggestions.description: In App.jsx, WorkerCard is passed `activityEntries={activityLog.filter(e => e.worker === w.name).slice(-3)}` — already capped at 3 items. Then inside WorkerCard: `const workerHistory = activityEntries.filter(e => e.worker === worker.name).slice(-3).reverse()`. The inner filter is redundant (parent already filtered), and the inner slice(-3) does nothing since there are already ≤3. Minor performance waste and confusing code duplication. Fix: remove the inner filter and slice from WorkerCard — trust the prop. If you ever want to show more history (e.g., 5 entries), you'd have to change two places. Pass more entries from the parent and let WorkerCard decide how many to show.

suggestions.title: No loading state in the Office tab — blank canvas on first load
suggestions.description: When the app loads, isLoading=true shows a skeleton in the Dashboard tab. But if you start on the Office tab (the default), you see an empty office floor with just Bloberto and no workers until the first fetch completes. There's no visual indicator that data is incoming. Add a subtle 'Syncing…' overlay or at minimum show ghost/placeholder desk silhouettes while `isLoading` is true in the Office view. The isSyncing prop is already passed to Office — wire it up.

suggestions.title: Activity log capped at 20 with no pagination or 'load more'
suggestions.description: In ActivityLog: `const displayed = [...entries].reverse().slice(0, 20)`. Twenty entries is fine day-to-day but after a busy session users have no way to see older history. The section header says 'Last N events' which hints at this limit. Add a 'Show more' button that expands to 50, or implement simple pagination (Prev/Next). Alternatively, increase the in-memory cap from 50 (currently `setActivityLog(prev => [{...}, ...prev].slice(0, 50))`) to 100 and let users paginate through it. The section header already dynamically shows the count — it's almost asking for this.

suggestions.title: Task tags are interactive but have no visible focus ring description
suggestions.description: Task tag spans in WorkerCard are rendered with `role='button'` and `tabIndex={0}` when `onTagClick` is provided. They respond to Enter/Space keypresses (good). However, the `title` attribute is used for the tooltip text (`Filter by: QA`), which is not reliably announced by screen readers. The active filter state (`task-tag--active` class + ' ×' suffix) provides visual feedback but no `aria-pressed` or `aria-label` update. Fix: add `aria-pressed={isActive}` to active tags, and replace the title with `aria-label` for the filter action text.

suggestions.title: Vibe history localStorage access in useEffect has no error handling
suggestions.description: The vibe history effect does: `const raw = localStorage.getItem(VIBE_HISTORY_KEY); const history = raw ? JSON.parse(raw) : []`. The JSON.parse is wrapped in try/catch (good!), but the localStorage.getItem and setItem calls are not. In Safari private browsing and some hardened browser configurations, localStorage access throws a SecurityError. This would crash the effect silently only if inside try/catch — but the getItem/setItem calls are outside it. Wrap the entire localStorage block in try/catch, not just the parse. Same applies to the theme and shortcut-shown storage reads in the state initializers.

suggestions.title: Footer tagline counter '2/9' is confusing to non-developers
suggestions.description: The footer shows a small `{n}/{total}` counter next to the tagline (e.g., '2/9'). This is a cute dev easter egg but looks like a bug or a pagination artifact to regular users. The counter increments even when toggling between the vibe tagline and bonus taglines, making the math non-obvious. Either remove the counter or make it part of the interaction copy (e.g., 'tap for more ✦ 2/9' as a subtle tooltip rather than inline text). The opacity-0.55 styling shows the team knows it's secondary info — lean into that by making it a tooltip-only element.

suggestions.title: WallClock can be up to 60 seconds behind on initial render
suggestions.description: WallClock initializes with `useState(() => new Date())` and ticks every 60s. If the page loads at HH:MM:45, the clock won't update to HH:MM+1 until 60s later (actually up to ~105s depending on when the interval fires). Fix: calculate the milliseconds until the next whole minute and use that as the initial timeout, then switch to a 60s interval. `const msToNextMinute = 60000 - (Date.now() % 60000); setTimeout(() => { setTime(new Date()); setInterval(tick, 60000) }, msToNextMinute)`. Small detail, but it's a clock — accuracy is the whole point.

suggestions.title: Conference table 'last huddle' label is nearly invisible
suggestions.description: The last-huddle display (who was in the conference table and when) renders with `opacity: 0.5, fontSize: '0.38rem'` — that's 0.38rem, roughly 6px. It is literally unreadable without zooming in. Either remove it (it's a nice idea but not useful at that size), bump it to 0.6rem with a tooltip for details, or show it only on hover/focus of the conference table. The feature exists but is currently invisible in practice.

suggestions.title: TASK_TAG_DEFS and getTaskTags defined at module level but used only in WorkerCard
suggestions.description: TASK_TAG_DEFS array and getTaskTags function are defined at module scope in App.jsx between utility functions and components. They're only ever called inside WorkerCard. While not a bug, this scatters component-specific logic into the module scope, making the file harder to navigate (it's already 1229 lines). Consider co-locating them near WorkerCard, or extracting WorkerCard + its helpers into a separate WorkerCard.jsx file. Same goes for StatusBadge, ProgressBar, LoadingSkeleton, EmptyState, and ActivityLog — all solid extraction candidates.

suggestions.title: Error state workers missing from StatsBar 'On the Clock' count — misleading total
suggestions.description: StatsBar: `const total = workers.length`. Since `activeWorkers` in App.jsx filters for `status === 'working' || status === 'idle' || status === 'error'`, workers in error state ARE included in the total. But the label says '👥 On the Clock' which implies active presence. An erroring worker is arguably not 'on the clock' productively. More importantly, the error count card only appears conditionally (`errorCount > 0 && ...`) which causes a layout shift when errors appear or clear. Pre-render it with zero and hide with visibility:hidden, or always include it in the grid as a faded zero state.
